subreq_desc: Named oversight body with responsibilities in ToR/charters
evidence_ref: AR2025 OFR 9.8 p.40 & CGS p.87-100; CTAP2024 Board-approved
gap_note: Board plus all four standing Committees oversee climate; Sustainability Committee has explicit climate remit; CTAP 2024 Board-approved. Strong.

subreq_desc: Oversight body skills/competencies to govern climate
gap_note: Board skills matrix exists but climate-specific competency disclosure not confirmed in reviewed sections; verify in CGS.

subreq_desc: How and how often the body is informed
gap_note: Climate a standing Board/Committee agenda item; reported to Sustainability Committee annually; RAC monitors risk.
Board approves CTAP and oversees targets; Capital Allocation Framework prioritises decarbonisation projects. Trade-off disclosure moderate.

subreq_desc: Oversight of targets + link to remuneration
evidence_source: BHP Annual Report 2025 (Remuneration Report; social value scorecard)
evidence_ref: AR2025 Rem Report p.104-112; OFR 9.8 p.41
gap_note: Board oversees GHG targets; decarbonisation pillar sits in social value scorecard linked to incentives. Exact % of incentive tied to climate not confirmed.

subreq_desc: Management role and integration with functions
gap_note: Management roles described; integration with planning/risk functions disclosed at OFR level.

subreq_desc: Specific climate risks/opportunities identified (physical vs transition)
gap_note: Transition and physical risks plus opportunities identified over short/medium/long term. Strong but pre-AASB framing.

subreq_desc: Time horizons defined and linked to planning
gap_note: Short/medium/long-term horizons used; explicit year-band definitions moderate.

subreq_desc: Business model/value chain effects and concentration
gap_note: Detailed value-chain analysis; Scope 3 concentration (84% steelmaking) disclosed.

subreq_desc: Strategy/decision-making + transition plan with assumptions and progress
gap_note: Board-approved CTAP 2024 is a full transition plan; OFR 9.8 reports FY2025 progress and changes since CTAP, including technology-delay impacts. Strong.

subreq_desc: Financial effects quantified + reflected in financial planning
gap_note: Decarbonisation spend quantified (~US$50m FY2025; ~US$0.5bn to FY2030; ~US$4bn in 2030s) and note 16 addresses some FS impacts, but broader financial-effect quantification (position/performance/cash flows) remains limited. Common gap.

subreq_desc: Climate resilience via scenario analysis (1.5C + high-warming)
evidence_ref: CTAP2024 p.34-37 & p.62; AR2025 TCFD table p.40
gap_note: Models a detailed 1.5C scenario with assumptions/signposts/benchmarks and references well-below-2C. Coverage of a high-warming (>2.5C) physical scenario per AASB S2 not confirmed; verify.

subreq_desc: Processes to identify and assess climate risks (inputs/scenarios)
gap_note: Group Risk Framework with scenario inputs; processes disclosed.
Uses Key Risk Indicators, risk appetite and maximum-foreseeable-loss scenarios.
Climate treated as a material risk within the common enterprise Risk Framework.

subreq_desc: Monitoring and changes from prior period
gap_note: RAC monitoring; OFR 9.8 explicitly describes changes to pathway since CTAP 2024.
Risk Framework treats risks as both threats and opportunities.
Climate risk processes integrated into the Group Risk Framework; integration disclosed.
Operational Scope 1+2 disclosed (8.7 MtCO2-e FY2025); Scope 1 split in Databook; GHG methodology document published.
Scope 2 disclosed; confirm both location-based and market-based bases in Databook.
Full Scope 3 inventory (~378 MtCO2-e FY2025) disclosed by category (Cat 10/11/15 etc.); 84% from iron ore + steelmaking coal processing. Unusually complete for the sector.
Transition risk discussed qualitatively with portfolio analysis; amount/% of assets vulnerable not quantified in AASB S2 form.
Physical climate risk approach and management disclosed; amount/% of assets vulnerable not quantified.
Opportunities (future-facing commodities, steel decarbonisation tech) described; % of assets aligned not quantified.
Climate capex/opex quantified (~US$50m FY2025; ~US$0.5bn to FY2030; ~US$4bn 2030s; CIP US$400m to FY2025). Clear figures; methodology label moderate.

Discloses use of regional internal carbon price assumptions in planning/investment/valuations since the 2010s and a carbon price protocol; specific price per tonne not clearly disclosed in reviewed text.

subreq_desc: Climate factored into executive remuneration (% linked)
gap_note: Decarbonisation milestone within social value scorecard tied to incentives; explicit percentage of executive incentive linked to climate not confirmed in reviewed sections.
Databook maps disclosures to multiple frameworks including SASB; sector metrics present.
Operational target -30% by FY2030 vs FY2020 baseline; net zero operational CY2050. Clear baseline and period; interim milestones limited (non-linear pathway).

Operational target met via structural abatement not offsets (explicit) and aligned to a 1.5C scenario - a genuine strength. But Scope 3 is only a long-term net zero GOAL described as uncertain, with no hard Scope 3 reduction target. Key gap.
Performance reported (36% below FY2020 baseline FY2025); independent assurance section (9.14) and assured sustainability data. Solid.

subreq_desc: Named oversight body with responsibilities in ToR/charters
gap_note: Board plus Sustainability Committee and Audit & Risk Committee oversee climate; charters and management accountabilities clearly disclosed.

gap_note: Board industry experience, technical knowledge and access to expertise cited; a formal climate skills matrix not explicitly confirmed.

subreq_desc: How and how often the body is informed
gap_note: ELT informed via presentations/Board papers/periodic updates; strategic risks reviewed by Board, ARC and ELT at least twice a year.

subreq_desc: Oversight of targets + link to remuneration
gap_note: Board oversees GHG targets; climate metrics are an explicit 15% of the Company Scorecard in Variable Annual Reward (gross Scope 1+2 + new-energy progress).

subreq_desc: Management role and integration with functions
gap_note: CEO accountable; EVP Sustainability on ELT; VP Climate; cross-functional integration into the risk management process disclosed.

subreq_desc: Specific climate risks/opportunities identified (physical vs transition)
gap_note: Discloses current and anticipated material physical risks, transition risks and transition opportunities across the business and value chain.

subreq_desc: Time horizons defined and linked to planning
gap_note: Risks assessed over short/medium/long term and against cash flows, access to finance and cost of capital; explicit year-band definitions to confirm.

subreq_desc: Business model/value chain effects and concentration
gap_note: Business model and value chain section; LNG demand scenarios; concentration in LNG/gas disclosed.

subreq_desc: Strategy/decision-making + transition plan with assumptions and progress
evidence_source: Woodside Annual Report 2025 + Climate & Sustainability Summary
gap_note: Climate strategy integrated (reduce Scope 1+2; invest in transition); asset decarbonisation plans and progress reported. Net zero 2050 is an aspiration, not a committed Paris-aligned plan.

subreq_desc: Financial effects quantified + reflected in financial planning
gap_note: Risks assessed against cash flows/finance/cost of capital with financial-impact assessment where information sufficient; internal carbon price US$80/t used in capital allocation. Stronger than most, still partial.

subreq_desc: Climate resilience via scenario analysis (1.5C + high-warming)
gap_note: Investment resilience tested via scenario analysis incl. 1.5C pathways. Disclosure of a high-warming (>2.5C) physical scenario per AASB S2 two-scenario rule not confirmed; verify in s3.6.8.

subreq_desc: Processes to identify and assess climate risks (inputs/scenarios)
gap_note: Risk management process (s3.7) with climate risk identification process in Basis of Preparation (s3.6.8.1); scenario analysis used.
Risks categorised strategic/emerging/current; Board sets risk appetite per strategic risk; assessed against financial factors.
Materiality assessment prioritises topics; strategic risks (incl. climate) reviewed by the Board.

subreq_desc: Monitoring and changes from prior period
gap_note: Strategic risks reviewed at least twice yearly; new material topic identified for 2026, showing live monitoring.
Transition opportunities assessed alongside risks within the same process.
Sustainability/climate risks explicitly incorporated into the enterprise risk management process. Clear integration.
Gross equity Scope 1+2 = 6,616 kt CO2-e (2.5% below 2024); Scope 1 disclosed.

evidence_source: Woodside Annual Report 2025 / climate data table
gap_note: Scope 2 disclosed on equity basis; confirm both location-based and market-based.
Scope 3 emissions disclosed (dominated by use of sold product); category granularity to confirm in the climate data table.
Transition risk to an LNG-growth portfolio discussed qualitatively; amount/% of assets vulnerable not quantified in AASB S2 form.
Physical climate risk identified; amount/% of assets vulnerable not quantified.
New-energy investment quantified ($5bn target) but % of assets aligned not expressed in the cross-industry metric form.
Scope 3 investment target US$5bn by 2030; Beaumont New Ammonia US$2.35bn; decarbonisation capex disclosed.
Explicit internal carbon price US$80/t CO2-e (real 2024) applied in capital allocation and abatement decisions; methane priced at ~US$6,720/t (x84 GWP). Strong.

subreq_desc: Climate factored into executive remuneration (% linked)
gap_note: Climate = explicit 15% of Company Scorecard (gross Scope 1+2 performance + new-energy progress), excluding offsets. Clear and quantified.
Reports against Ipieca (oil & gas), IOGP and GRI; sector metrics present; SASB mapping to confirm.
Net equity Scope 1+2 -30% by 2030 vs starting base; methane <0.2% to 2029; Scope 3 5 Mtpa abatement + US$5bn investment by 2030. Baselines/periods defined; interim annual.

Transparent on offsets: 1,283 kt credits retired to meet 5,334 kt net target; prioritises abatement first and excludes offsets from the scorecard. BUT the net Scope 1+2 target leans on offsets and there is NO absolute Scope 3 reduction target (only intensity/abatement/investment). Key gap.
2025 net target (5,334 kt) met; gross 6,616 kt externally assured. Performance tracked with external limited assurance.
